papers.title_en: Steering Code LLMs with Activation Directions for Language and Library Control
papers.authors: Md Mahbubur Rahman, Arjun Guha, Harshitha Menon
papers.evidence: 在代码生成过程中引导LLM进行特定库的控制
papers.tldr: 本研究探讨了代码大语言模型在生成代码时对特定编程语言和库的偏好。研究者发现这些偏好在模型的激活空间中表现为近似线性的方向。通过使用均值差分法提取转向向量并在推理时干预隐藏状态，研究成功引导了模型生成目标语言或库的代码，甚至在提示词要求相反时依然有效。这证明了代码风格偏好在激活空间中具有可控的紧凑结构，为精准控制代码生成提供了新方法。
papers.abstract_en: Code LLMs often default to particular programming languages and libraries under neutral prompts. We investigate whether these preferences are encoded as approximately linear directions in activation space that can be manipulated at inference time. Using a difference-in-means method, we estimate layer-wise steering vectors for five language/library pairs and add them to model hidden states during generation. Across three open-weight code LLMs, these interventions substantially increase generation toward the target ecosystem under neutral prompts and often remain effective even when prompts explicitly request the opposite choice. Steering strength varies by model and target, with common ecosystems easier to induce than rarer alternatives, and overly strong interventions can reduce output quality. Overall, our results suggest that code-style preferences in LLMs are partly represented by compact, steerable structure in activation space.

papers.title_en: The Kitchen Loop: User-Spec-Driven Development for a Self-Evolving Codebase
papers.evidence: LLM代理作为合成超级用户执行规范接口
papers.tldr: 本研究提出 Kitchen Loop 框架，旨在解决软件开发中“构建什么”及“验证有效性”的瓶颈。该框架通过统一信任模型实现代码库的自主演进，核心包含规格说明、LLM 模拟的高频用户测试、不可伪造的基准测试及漂移控制。在生产系统验证中，该框架经 285 次迭代生成千余个 PR 且无回归错误，展现了自我修复和质量持续提升的特性，为自主软件演进提供了安全的操作规范。
papers.abstract_en: Code production is now a commodity; the bottleneck is knowing what to build and proving it works. We present the Kitchen Loop, a framework for autonomous, self-evolving software built on a unified trust model: (1) a specification surface enumerating what the product claims to support; (2) 'As a User x 1000', where an LLM agent exercises that surface as a synthetic power user at 1,000x human cadence; (3) Unbeatable Tests, ground-truth verification the code author cannot fake; and (4) Drift Control, continuous quality measurement with automated pause gates. We validate across two production systems over 285+ iterations, producing 1,094+ merged pull requests with zero regressions detected by the regression oracle (methodology in Section 6.1). We observe emergent properties at scale: multi-iteration self-correction chains, autonomous infrastructure healing, and monotonically improving quality gates. The primitives are not new; our contribution is their composition into a production-tested system with the operational discipline that makes long-running autonomous evolution safe.
